focus: D1 Correctness - phase 001 precedence swap and phase 002 rerank sidecar correctness

findingDetails.title: Sidecar pytest contract does not prove the edge behavior it names
findingDetails.evidence: The sigmoid bounds test only checks one live API response for [0,1], the concurrent serialization test only verifies all gathered requests return bounded scores, and the SIGTERM test checks process exit without observing lifespan cleanup.
findingDetails.recommendation: Add deterministic unit coverage for sigmoid extreme logits, an instrumented fake predict() counter proving max_active == 1 under concurrent requests, and observable lifespan cleanup evidence on SIGTERM.
findingDetails.scopeProof: The full sidecar pytest file was reviewed; these are the only tests covering sigmoid bounds, concurrent serialization, and SIGTERM behavior.

noveltyJustification: One new P2 test-isolation advisory was found in the first correctness pass; no prior registry entries existed, so weighted new findings ratio is 1/1.
traceabilityChecks: Phase 001 precedence swap verified in stage3-rerank.ts lines 379-472.
Phase 002 sidecar sigmoid, lock, health, warmup, rerank, SIGTERM launcher, and tests inspected.
edgeCases: Uvicorn lifespan-on-SIGTERM is an external runtime contract; local launcher exec routing was verified, but no live instrumentation proved the lifespan cleanup block executed.
Sidecar pytest suite uses live happy-path requests and is weak for deterministic edge contracts.
integrationEvidence: stage3-rerank.ts cross-encoder branch returns before local branch
search-flags.ts local guard short-circuits when cross-encoder enabled
rerank_sidecar.py locks around predict and normalizes raw scores
start.sh execs uvicorn so SIGTERM reaches server process
